title: Power Factor Monitoring
dataSources: **Power meter SNMP (PQ meters)** paths normalize into **`index=power`** with **`sourcetype="power_meter:pq"`**, exposing extracted numeric **`pf`**, **`kw`**, **`kvar`**, and harmonic proxies mapped as **`thd`** / **`thd_pct`** plus dimensional **`feed_id`**. SNMP Modular Input deployments commonly cite Splunkbase 1537 when translating enterprise PQ MIB walks into those fields; validate OID-to-field mappings against each meter family before trusting thresholds.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="power_meter:pq"
| where pf < 0.92 OR thd_pct > 8
| timechart span=15m avg(pf) as avg_pf by feed_id
description: Monitors SNMP-polled PQ telemetry on main distribution feeds so Splunk surfaces sustained lagging power factor, harmonic distortion creep, and per-feed divergence across feed_id before breaker thermal margins or tariff clauses are breached.
value: Correcting lagging power factor avoids reactive-energy penalties and frees transformer capacity, while harmonic excursions accelerate copper and neutral heating; Splunk-backed PQ trending ties remediation spend directly to avoided utility demand ratchets and premature capacitor-bank replacement.
implementation: Configure SNMP polls so every monitored MSB feed lands **`feed_id`**, **`pf`**, **`thd_pct`**, **`kw`**, and **`kvar`** at least every five minutes into **`power_meter:pq`**. Align **`pf`** alert thresholds with the utility reactive-demand rider or internal PF guarantee (often 0.92 leading/lagging). Layer optional capacitor-step states only when that auxiliary sourcetype already exists—otherwise trend PF alone and annotate remediation tickets manually.
visualization: Dashboard Studio dark layout: background **`splunk.rectangle`** bands separating KPI strip from trends; **`splunk.singlevalue`** panels bind worst-case **`pf`** and **`thd_pct`** with **`majorField`** overrides; **`splunk.line`** charts plot **`avg_pf`** **`by feed_id`** using **`seriesColors`** aligned to facility branding; **`splunk.table`** lists breached **`feed_id`**, latest **`kw`**, **`kvar`**, and SLA countdown timers; optional **`splunk.markdown`** interprets tariff clauses beside the charts.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable — PQ SNMP metrics remain raw structured logs rather than accelerated CIM nodes for this observability UC.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — SNMP network monitoring overview

knownFalsePositives:
**PDU rack-and-stack brownouts on Cage-Reprovision weekends** produce single-interval PF dips—suppress via Splunk lookup `planned_pdu_work.csv` keyed on **`feed_id`** plus `_time` windows pulled from change tickets.
**Generator quarterly load-step drills on ATS-A** harmonics mimic distortion faults—exclude drills when CMMS **`activity_type=GEN_TEST`** overlaps the bucket.
**Chiller soft-start sequencing after chilled-water outages** spikes **`thd_pct`** briefly—require three consecutive **`span=15m`** breaches before paging unless **`avg_pf`** stays below contract simultaneously.
**SNMP agent restart jitter after MIBWalk QA** emits zero readings—hold alerts **`45 minutes`** post **`snmpd`** bounce using a **`lookup snmp_agent_restart`** flag tied to **`feed_id`**.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads grant **`power_ops`** (or equivalent facilities-engineering role) read rights on **`index=power`** plus capability to schedule alerts every fifteen minutes.
• Deploy **Power meter SNMP (PQ meters)** ingestion (cross-check Splunkbase 1537 SNMP Modular Input patterns when applicable) so SNMP modular inputs write **`sourcetype="power_meter:pq"`** events carrying **`pf`**, **`thd_pct`**, **`kw`**, **`kvar`**, and **`feed_id`**; capture props/transform notes in **`inputs.conf`** alongside any **`TRANSFORMS-`** stanza that normalizes harmonic counters.
• Document SNMP community or SNMPv3 `authPriv` secrets outside Splunk—reference **`docs/implementation-guide.md`** for directory layout on heavy forwarders versus Cloud Collect.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Poll main switchboards and PDU incomers at **`300` seconds** or faster during commissioning, relaxing toward **`900` seconds** when harmonic drift stabilizes.
• Stamp **`feed_id`** consistently with electrical one-line labels (for example **`MSB-L3-A`** versus **`PDU-HPC-POD21`**).
• When capacitor banks emit auxiliary SNMP traps or discrete registers, mirror those inputs under a sibling sourcetype before correlating bank switching noise with PF dips.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Reuse the catalogue SPL unchanged—saved-search **`dispatch.earliest_time=-35m@m`** pairs cleanly with **`cron_schedule=*/15 * * * *`** so each fifteen-minute **`span=15m`** bucket completes before evaluation.
Pin **`relation`** alerts to utility-contract **`pf`** floors (often **`0.92`**) and harmonic ceilings aligned with **`thd_pct`** distortion budgets negotiated with the transmission operator.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare Splunk **`avg_pf`** for each **`feed_id`** against the handheld PQ analyzer or rack **`PDU`** dashboard during the same **`±15m`** window to confirm SNMP scaling factors.
Spot-check **`kw`** versus **`kvar`** triangles so **`pf`** recomputes manually within published meter tolerance.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish a Dashboard Studio row combining **`splunk.line`** PF trends, **`splunk.singlevalue`** gauges for worst **`feed_id`**, and **`splunk.table`** listings sorted by harmonic severity.
Route Sev2 pages through webhook routing rules tied to electrical supervisor queues with **`alert.suppress.fields=feed_id`** set to **`30m`** minimum.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot common failures
• **Failure:** SNMP modular input timeouts yield **`missing pf`** extractions—inspect **`splunkd.log`** on the polling worker for **`SNMP`** handshake retries and widen **`timeout`** seconds before blaming meters.
• **Failure:** **`role`** **`permission`** misconfiguration hides **`index=power`** during scheduled searches—grant `indexes_edit` viewers read-only clones instead of sharing admin passwords.
• **Failure:** Firmware refreshes reorder MIB branches producing **`NULL thd_pct`**—reload transforms and replay baseline **`feed_id`** samples until Splunk matches vendor PQ screenshots.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Replay fifteen-minute PQ samples where **`feed_id=MSB-L3-A`** holds **`pf=0.87`** and **`thd_pct=10`** across four contiguous buckets while **`kw`** stays positive; the saved correlation search must breach threshold, emit an alert payload referencing **`avg_pf`**, and retain drill-down rows for auditors.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Inject one noisy bucket where **`pf`** dips to **`0.905`** during a sixty-second motor-start transient but neighbouring buckets remain above **`0.96`** with flat harmonics; automation must stay silent yet still log the blip inside the **`timechart`** for trending review.

evidence: Export the scheduled **`PQ-PowerFactor-Breach`** alert plus backing **`timechart`** panel screenshot showing **`index=power`** **`power_meter:pq`** **`feed_id`** drilldown retained **`90` days** in the ops evidence SharePoint.
exclusions: Does not certify IEEE-compliant harmonic mitigation designs, replace calibrated PQ meters, or close utility tariff disputes—Splunk only evidences telemetry breaches requiring electrical engineers to act in the field.
